what_it_does: Defines a 'Gallery' screen for the platform UI kit: a title, an ActionToolbar (select/create/upload/search), a grid/list view toggle, a grid of 15 selectable image media tiles, and a grid of 5 video tiles. Carries minimal interactive state (which tiles are selected, grid vs list) and is exposed on window.Gallery.
observations: Uses React hooks via destructuring from the global React (`const { useState: useState_g } = React;`) — aliased to avoid name collisions in a shared bundle.
Two useState hooks: a Set of selected tile indices (default {0,6,8}) and a view mode ('grid'/'list').
toggle() clones the Set, adds/removes the index, and sets state (immutable update pattern).
Renders 15 image tiles and 5 video tiles via Array.from(...).map with keys.
References an external ActionToolbar component not defined in this file (expected to be a global/sibling).
MediaTile draws an inline SVG play button for video, including a hardcoded rgba(31,26,18,.08) circle fill (a raw color literal).
view state toggles the active class but both view modes render the same tile-grid (list view not visually implemented).
Attached to global via `window.Gallery = Gallery;` rather than module export.
standouts: It is the interactive member of the kit — actual selection state and a view toggle, unlike the static Calendar screen.
The aliasing of useState to useState_g signals a shared global-namespace bundle where multiple screens coexist.
conventions: Follows the kit's global-window registration and class-only styling conventions.
Uses immutable Set updates for React state (idiomatic).
Breaks the no-raw-color rule once: rgba(31,26,18,.08) inline in the video play SVG.
Depends on an undeclared external ActionToolbar — relies on global load order rather than imports.
concerns: List view is selectable but not implemented — toggling to 'list' changes nothing visible.
Hardcoded rgba(31,26,18,.08) bypasses the token system.
ActionToolbar is referenced but undefined here; will throw if the global isn't loaded first.
notes: An interactive gallery-screen mockup with tile selection and a (cosmetic-only) grid/list toggle, depending on a global ActionToolbar and registered on window.Gallery.
questions: Where is ActionToolbar defined and is it guaranteed to load before this file?
Is the list view intended to render differently, or is the toggle purely decorative for the mockup?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches: it presents a gallery screen and is interactive, though the 'list' view it advertises is not actually rendered.
intention_signals: Provides a media-gallery screen for the platform kit with enough interactivity (selection, view toggle) to demonstrate the design's behavior, not just its static look.
novelty: Among the kit screens it carries real React state and an immutable-Set selection model, making it the interactive exemplar.
contribution: Brings a media-gallery screen layout plus a selectable-tile interaction pattern and a grid/list view-toggle control to the platform kit.
summary_for_embedding: An interactive React gallery-screen mockup in the platform UI kit with selectable image tiles, video tiles, an action toolbar, and a grid/list view toggle, registered on window.Gallery.
intention_for_embedding: To present a media-gallery screen for the platform kit with demonstrable tile-selection and view-toggle interactivity.
